domain: Inuit inuksuk stacked-stone navigation marker (Canadian Arctic): cement-free balanced stacks; 3 types nalunaikkutaq (single upright deconfuser) + tikkuuti (triangular pointer) + inunnguaq (human-figure); chained sight-line navigation; depth/safety/cache/direction info via geometric type.
specific_mechanism: Physical-balance spatial marker with 3 geometric type variants each encoding different semantic scale; chained sight-line for route traversal.
llm_application: INUKSUK 3-TYPE geometric output-diagnostic ladder: define 3 graded eval primitives: (1) NALUNAIKKUTAQ: single-token Y/N 'on-task' check — cheap, runs every step; (2) TIKKUUTI: 2-3 token next-step direction check — moderate, runs every 10 steps; (3) INUNNGUAQ: full multi-token output sample — expensive, runs at completion. Geometric token-count complexity = diagnostic depth proxy. Chain: upper levels imply lower (full output contains direction contains on-task). Differs from (a) per-token entropy (single-dim), (b) HELM multi-axis (no chain), (c) PRAYER-FLAG 5-axis (different axes not graded), (d) test-time compute scaling (compute not diagnostic type) by combining (i) 3-LEVEL GRADED primitives + (ii) TOKEN-COUNT complexity proxy + (iii) CHAIN-IMPLIES across levels.
novelty_claim: Per-token entropy single-dim. HELM many axes no chain. Test-time compute scaling generic compute. INUKSUK recombines (a) 3-LEVEL graded eval + (b) TOKEN-COUNT complexity proxy + (c) CHAIN-IMPLIES levels. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
